content.text: Identity Subscription

The identity subscription is the platform subscription that hosts centralized authentication infrastructure for hybrid workloads: either Entra ID Domain Services (managed domain controllers) for cloud-only or lift-and-shift scenarios, or customer-managed Windows Server Active Directory domain controllers for organizations that require on-premises AD running in Azure. This subscription is isolated from workload subscriptions so that identity infrastructure cannot be modified by application teams. Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) itself is a tenant-level service and is not deployed here — the subscription hosts the Azure resources that extend on-premises AD into Azure.

attrs.alt: Identity subscription architecture showing Entra ID sync, AADDS, and VNet peering to hub

content.text: Entra ID (the cloud directory) is always available regardless of what is in the identity subscription. The identity subscription is only needed when workloads require domain join (Kerberos/NTLM authentication) or LDAP access to an AD domain — for example, Windows VMs that need Group Policy, SQL Server Windows auth, or legacy applications using Integrated Windows Authentication.
Identity Architecture Decision
Scenario
Identity approach
What lives in identity subscription
Cloud-only (no on-premises)
Entra ID + Entra Domain Services (AADDS)
AADDS managed domain (no DCs to manage), identity VNet, NSG
Hybrid with AD Connect sync
On-premises AD is primary; AADDS or IaaS DCs in Azure for local auth
Entra Domain Services OR 2+ Windows Server DC VMs, identity VNet, proximity placement group
Hybrid, extending AD to Azure (IaaS DCs)
Customer-managed Windows Server DCs in the identity subscription
DC-01, DC-02 VMs in separate availability zones, AD replication, SYSVOL backup
Cloud-only, no domain join needed
Entra ID only (no identity subscription needed)
Nothing — identity subscription is not required for Entra ID-only workloads
Deploying Entra ID Domain Services

Entra ID Domain Services (AADDS) provides managed domain controllers — Microsoft runs the VMs, patches them, and replicates the directory. AADDS syncs all cloud user accounts from Entra ID into a managed AD domain. Workloads in spoke VNets that peer to the hub can domain-join and authenticate via this domain. The NSG on the DC subnet must allow inbound TCP 443 and 5986 from the AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices service tag — this is required for Microsoft to manage the controllers and is blocked by the default Deny rules otherwise.

# Deploy Entra ID Domain Services (Azure AD DS) in the identity subscription
# Provides managed domain controllers — no VMs to patch.
# Requires Entra ID P2 license for all users who will use domain services.

SUB_ID='<identity-sub-id>'
RG='rg-identity-prod-eastus'
LOCATION='eastus'
DOMAIN='contoso.corp'

# Create the identity resource group
az group create --name $RG --location $LOCATION --subscription $SUB_ID

# Create the identity VNet
az network vnet create \
  --name vnet-identity-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --address-prefix 10.1.0.0/24 \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Subnet for domain controllers (AADDS requires /24 or smaller subnet)
az network vnet subnet create \
  --name snet-dc-prod-eastus \
  --vnet-name vnet-identity-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --address-prefix 10.1.0.0/27 \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# NSG for the DC subnet — Entra Domain Services requires specific inbound rules
az network nsg create \
  --name nsg-dc-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Required inbound rules for Entra Domain Services management traffic
az network nsg rule create \
  --name 'AllowAADDSHTTPS' \
  --nsg-name nsg-dc-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --priority 101 \
  --access Allow \
  --protocol Tcp \
  --direction Inbound \
  --source-address-prefixes AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices \
  --source-port-ranges '*' \
  --destination-port-ranges 443 5986 \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Associate NSG to DC subnet
az network vnet subnet update \
  --name snet-dc-prod-eastus \
  --vnet-name vnet-identity-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --nsg nsg-dc-prod-eastus \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

SUBNET_ID=$(az network vnet subnet show \
  --name snet-dc-prod-eastus \
  --vnet-name vnet-identity-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --query id -o tsv)

# Deploy Entra Domain Services (20-40 minutes)
az ds domain-service create \
  --name $DOMAIN \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --subnet-id $SUBNET_ID \
  --sku Standard \
  --filtered-sync Enabled \
  --location $LOCATION \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Get DC IPs after provisioning (needed for spoke DNS configuration)
az ds domain-service show \
  --name $DOMAIN \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --query 'domainControllerIpAddress' -o tsv
Configuring Spoke VNets to Use Domain Controller DNS

For spoke workloads to domain-join and resolve the AD domain, their DNS queries must reach the domain controllers. With Azure Firewall DNS proxy enabled (which is required for private DNS zone resolution), the cleanest approach is to update the Firewall Policy's DNS servers to point to the DC IPs — all spokes that send DNS queries to the Firewall automatically use the DCs without per-spoke VNet changes. Alternatively, each spoke VNet can have custom DNS servers set directly to the DC IPs, but this requires updating every spoke individually and does not compose with the Firewall DNS proxy.

# Configure spoke VNets to use identity subscription DCs as custom DNS servers
# This enables domain-join and Kerberos authentication from spoke workloads.
# DNS query path: spoke VM -> Firewall DNS proxy -> DC IPs (via identity peering)

# DC IPs from Entra Domain Services (replace with actual values)
DC_IP1='10.1.0.4'
DC_IP2='10.1.0.5'

# Option A: Set custom DNS at the Azure Firewall DNS proxy level
# (Preferred: one change, all spokes benefit via the Firewall DNS proxy)
FW_POLICY='afwp-hub-prod-eastus'
RG_CONN='rg-connectivity-prod-eastus'
SUB_CONN='<connectivity-sub-id>'

az network firewall policy update \
  --name $FW_POLICY \
  --resource-group $RG_CONN \
  --dns-servers $DC_IP1 $DC_IP2 \
  --enable-dns-proxy true \
  --subscription $SUB_CONN

echo "Firewall DNS proxy now forwards to: $DC_IP1, $DC_IP2"

# Option B: Set custom DNS directly on each spoke VNet
# (Use only if not using Firewall DNS proxy)
SUB_APP='<payments-sub-id>'
RG_APP='rg-payments-network-prod-eastus'

az network vnet update \
  --name vnet-payments-prod-eastus \
  --resource-group $RG_APP \
  --dns-servers $DC_IP1 $DC_IP2 \
  --subscription $SUB_APP

# After changing DNS servers, restart all VMs in the VNet to pick up new DNS
# (DNS changes are applied on next DHCP lease renewal)
az vm restart \
  --resource-group $RG_APP \
  --subscription $SUB_APP \
  --ids $(az vm list --resource-group $RG_APP --subscription $SUB_APP --query '[].id' -o tsv)
PIM for Identity Subscription Access

The identity subscription requires the strictest access controls in the platform — a compromised identity subscription means compromised authentication for all workloads. No standing Owner or Contributor assignments should exist. The identity team uses PIM eligible assignments and activates access for specific tasks with justification and approval. The eligible assignment has no expiration (permanent eligibility) but each activation is time-limited (maximum 8 hours) and requires justification.

# Configure Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for identity subscription access
# PIM is managed in Entra ID, not via az CLI directly.
# These commands use az rest to call the PIM API.

SUB_ID='<identity-sub-id>'
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token \
  --resource 'https://management.azure.com' \
  --query accessToken -o tsv)

# List current eligible role assignments on the identity subscription
curl -s \
  "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilitySchedules?api-version=2022-04-01-preview" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -m json.tool | \
  python3 -c "import json,sys; data=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(i['properties']['roleDefinitionId'], i['properties']['principalId']) for i in data.get('value',[])]"

# Create a PIM eligible assignment: identity team as Contributor (time-limited)
IDENTITY_TEAM_OID='<identity-team-group-oid>'
CONTRIBUTOR_ROLE_ID='b24988ac-6180-42a0-ab88-20f7382dd24c'
REQUEST_ID=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests/${REQUEST_ID}?api-version=2022-04-01-preview" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{
    \"properties\": {
      \"roleDefinitionId\": \"/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/$CONTRIBUTOR_ROLE_ID\",
      \"principalId\": \"$IDENTITY_TEAM_OID\",
      \"requestType\": \"AdminAssign\",
      \"scheduleInfo\": {
        \"startDateTime\": null,
        \"expiration\": {\"type\": \"NoExpiration\"}
      },
      \"justification\": \"Identity platform team needs eligible access for DC management\"
    }
  }" | python3 -m json.tool | grep -E '\"(name|status|type)\"'

# Verify no standing Owner/Contributor assignments exist (should return nothing)
az role assignment list \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$SUB_ID" \
  --query '[?roleDefinitionName==`Contributor` || roleDefinitionName==`Owner`]' \
  --query '[].{Role:roleDefinitionName, Principal:principalName}' \
  -o table
Identity Subscription Policy
Policy
Scope
Effect
Rationale
Deny public IP on identity resources
alz-platform-identity MG
Deny
Domain controllers must never be internet-accessible; all management via Bastion in hub
Require NSG on all subnets
alz-platform-identity MG
Deny
NSG with AADDS required rules must be present before AADDS deploys
Deny VM extensions not in allow-list
alz-platform-identity MG
Deny
Prevents unauthorized software installation on DCs
Enable just-in-time VM access
alz-platform-identity MG
Modify/AuditIfNotExists
JIT access via Defender for Cloud reduces DC attack surface
Enable Defender for Servers on all VMs
alz-platform-identity MG
DeployIfNotExists
Endpoint detection and response (EDR) on DCs is mandatory
Require zone-redundant VM deployment
alz-platform-identity MG
Deny
DCs must be in separate availability zones for HA
IaaS Domain Controllers (Customer-Managed)

When AADDS is insufficient — for example when the organization requires custom AD schema extensions, fine-grained password policies with more than two tiers, Read-Only DCs for remote offices, or extremely low-latency AD responses — customer-managed Windows Server DCs run as IaaS VMs in the identity subscription. Two DCs in separate availability zones provide HA. The data disk (32 GB or larger, None caching) hosts NTDS.DIT and SYSVOL. Static private IPs are mandatory for DCs. Backup via Azure Backup with a DC-consistent backup policy is required.

# Deploy IaaS domain controllers (customer-managed) in the identity subscription
# Use this when AADDS is not sufficient (custom schema, fine-grained pwd policies,
# Read-Only DCs, or very low-latency AD requirements).

SUB_ID='<identity-sub-id>'
RG='rg-identity-prod-eastus'
LOCATION='eastus'
VNET='vnet-identity-prod-eastus'
SUBNET='snet-dc-prod-eastus'

# Deploy DC-01 in Availability Zone 1
az vm create \
  --name dc-prod-eastus-01 \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --location $LOCATION \
  --image 'Win2022Datacenter' \
  --size Standard_D2s_v5 \
  --zone 1 \
  --vnet-name $VNET \
  --subnet $SUBNET \
  --private-ip-address 10.1.0.4 \
  --nsg nsg-dc-prod-eastus \
  --public-ip-address '' \
  --admin-username azureadmin \
  --admin-password '<use Key Vault secret>' \
  --storage-sku Premium_LRS \
  --os-disk-caching ReadWrite \
  --data-disk-sizes-gb 32 \
  --data-disk-caching None \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Deploy DC-02 in Availability Zone 2 (for HA)
az vm create \
  --name dc-prod-eastus-02 \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --location $LOCATION \
  --image 'Win2022Datacenter' \
  --size Standard_D2s_v5 \
  --zone 2 \
  --vnet-name $VNET \
  --subnet $SUBNET \
  --private-ip-address 10.1.0.5 \
  --nsg nsg-dc-prod-eastus \
  --public-ip-address '' \
  --admin-username azureadmin \
  --admin-password '<use Key Vault secret>' \
  --storage-sku Premium_LRS \
  --os-disk-caching ReadWrite \
  --data-disk-sizes-gb 32 \
  --data-disk-caching None \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Enable Defender for Servers on DCs
az security atp storage update \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --is-enabled true

# Lock down the DC VMs: no public IPs, no JIT access via internet
# Access DCs only via Azure Bastion in the hub VNet
echo 'DCs deployed without public IPs. Use Bastion in hub VNet for management.'

# Set static private IP (IaaS DCs should have static IPs)
az network nic ip-config update \
  --name ipconfig1 \
  --nic-name $(az vm show --name dc-prod-eastus-01 --resource-group $RG \
               --subscription $SUB_ID --query 'networkProfile.networkInterfaces[0].id' -o tsv | \
               xargs az network nic show --ids --query name -o tsv) \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --private-ip-address 10.1.0.4 \
  --private-ip-address-version IPv4 \
  --allocation-method Static \
  --subscription $SUB_ID
Conditional Access for Platform Access

Conditional Access policies in Entra ID enforce MFA and device compliance before any user can activate their PIM eligible role on the identity subscription. The policy targets the platform team group and the Azure Management API application (797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013). Without Conditional Access, a user with a valid PIM eligible role could activate it from an unmanaged personal device — Conditional Access closes this gap by requiring both a satisfied MFA challenge and a device enrolled in Intune.

# Conditional Access policies for platform team access to the identity subscription
# Conditional Access is configured in Entra ID (tenant scope), not in the subscription.
# Use Microsoft Graph API or az rest to manage CA policies programmatically.

# Authenticate to Graph API
GRAPH_TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token \
  --resource 'https://graph.microsoft.com' \
  --query accessToken -o tsv)

# Create a Conditional Access policy: require MFA + compliant device for identity sub access
# This policy targets users with eligible PIM assignments to the identity subscription.
PLATFORM_TEAM_OID='<platform-team-group-oid>'

curl -s -X POST \
  'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies' \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAPH_TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{
    \"displayName\": \"Require MFA and compliant device for identity subscription access\",
    \"state\": \"enabled\",
    \"conditions\": {
      \"users\": {
        \"includeGroups\": [\"$PLATFORM_TEAM_OID\"]
      },
      \"applications\": {
        \"includeApplications\": [\"797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013\"]
      },
      \"clientAppTypes\": [\"all\"]
    },
    \"grantControls\": {
      \"operator\": \"AND\",
      \"builtInControls\": [\"mfa\", \"compliantDevice\"]
    }
  }" | python3 -m json.tool | grep -E '\"(id|displayName|state)\"'

# List all Conditional Access policies
curl -s 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies' \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAPH_TOKEN" | \
  python3 -c "import json,sys; [print(p['displayName'], '-', p['state']) for p in json.load(sys.stdin)['value']]"
Auditing the Identity Subscription

Five key audits apply to the identity subscription: DC VMs in separate availability zones (HA verification), no public IPs attached to any NIC (attack surface), presence of AADDS or DC VMs (confirming the domain exists), no standing Owner/Contributor role assignments (PIM compliance), and NSG rules allowing the AADDS service tag (operational requirement for managed DC management). These queries run weekly as part of the platform governance report.

// Resource Graph: audit identity subscription resources

// 1. Verify domain controller VMs are in separate availability zones
Resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| where subscriptionId =~ '<identity-sub-id>'
| extend zones = tostring(zones)
| project name, resourceGroup, zones, location
| order by name asc

// 2. Check for public IPs attached to NICs in the identity subscription
Resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.network/networkinterfaces'
| where subscriptionId =~ '<identity-sub-id>'
| mv-expand ipConfig = properties.ipConfigurations
| extend publicIpId = tostring(ipConfig.properties.publicIPAddress.id)
| where isnotempty(publicIpId)
| project name, resourceGroup, publicIpId

// 3. Verify Entra Domain Services or IaaS DCs are present
Resources
| where subscriptionId =~ '<identity-sub-id>'
| where type =~ 'microsoft.aad/domainservices'
    or (type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' and name startswith 'dc-')
| project name, type, resourceGroup, location

// 4. Role assignments in the identity subscription (detect standing access)
AuthorizationResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.authorization/roleassignments'
| where subscriptionId =~ '<identity-sub-id>'
| extend
    roleDefId   = tostring(properties.roleDefinitionId),
    principalId = tostring(properties.principalId),
    scope       = tostring(properties.scope)
| where roleDefId endswith '8e3af657-a8ff-443c-a75c-2fe8c4bcb635'  // Owner
    or roleDefId endswith 'b24988ac-6180-42a0-ab88-20f7382dd24c'  // Contributor
| project principalId, roleDefId, scope
| order by scope asc

// 5. NSG rules on the DC subnet — verify AADDS service tag rules exist
Resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.network/networksecuritygroups'
| where subscriptionId =~ '<identity-sub-id>'
| mv-expand rule = properties.securityRules
| extend
    ruleName = tostring(rule.name),
    srcAddrPrefix = tostring(rule.properties.sourceAddressPrefix),
    dstPort = tostring(rule.properties.destinationPortRange)
| where srcAddrPrefix =~ 'AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices'
| project name, ruleName, srcAddrPrefix, dstPort
Common Identity Subscription Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Fix
Deploying both DCs in the same availability zone
A zone failure takes down both DCs simultaneously; no AD auth possible
Deploy DC-01 in Zone 1 and DC-02 in Zone 2 (or Zone 3); use AZ-aware load balancing
Setting dynamic private IPs on DC NICs
After a VM restart, the DC gets a new IP; DNS clients can no longer reach it
Set static private IPs (10.1.0.4 and 10.1.0.5) on both DC NICs via NIC IP config
Not setting caching=None on DC data disk
OS caches writes to the data disk; AD database corruption can occur if the VM is force-stopped
Data disk (hosting NTDS.DIT and SYSVOL) must have caching=None; OS disk caching=ReadWrite is fine
Using AADDS for workloads that require on-premises AD GPOs
AADDS uses its own Group Policy objects; on-prem GPOs do not sync to AADDS
For GPO-dependent workloads, use IaaS DCs that replicate from on-premises and receive the same GPOs
Granting application teams access to the identity subscription
Application team credentials can be used to modify or delete DCs
Identity subscription is platform-only; no application team should have any role assignment here
Forgetting to update spoke VNet DNS after changing DC IPs
VMs use stale DC IPs; domain join fails and Kerberos auth breaks
After any DC IP change, update Firewall Policy DNS servers (or spoke VNet custom DNS) and restart VMs
Summary

The identity subscription hosts centralized authentication infrastructure under the alz-platform-identity management group. For most ESLZ deployments, Entra ID Domain Services provides managed domain controllers without the overhead of VM management. The DC subnet requires an NSG with inbound rules allowing the AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices service tag on ports 443 and 5986. Spoke workloads resolve the AD domain via the Firewall DNS proxy (which forwards to DC IPs) or via custom VNet DNS settings. No standing privileged access is permitted — the identity team uses PIM eligible assignments with time-limited activations.
